ref: This story will show you what the kingdom of heaven is like: One day a king decided to call in his officials and ask them to give an account of what they owed him.
text: Mo̱nꞌ nu ane̱ŋ ne̱ a du Wuribware̱ se̱ a kuwure‑o‑ro‑o. N gye̱ owure ko̱ ya dɛɛ kye̱na aaa? Ne̱ mò̱ ayaafɔre̱‑ɔ bo̱ko̱ de mò̱ ko̱kɔ. Ne̱ ɔɔ waa mò̱ mfɛɛre̱ fe̱yɛ ɔ te̱e̱ bamo̱ pɛɛɛ a ɔ bo̱ gyaŋŋe̱ na ɔ nya ŋu mò̱ aa bamo̱ mbo̱ŋtɔ‑rɔ atanne̱ ne̱ a bo̱‑rɔ‑ɔ na ɔ kɔɔre̱ le̱e̱ bamo̱ ase̱.
translated: The child who was crying went to Wuribware and said he was hungry. What could he do to get food? His mother had died and left him alone. If you see a child suffering like that, you should take pity on him and give him some food so that he can grow up and remember the kindness you showed him.

text: Owure a kpa a ɔ kɔɔre̱ atanne̱‑ɔ le̱e̱ mò̱ ase̱‑ɔ, ne̱ ɔ yɛ ɔ maa taare̱ a ɔ ka. Amo̱se̱‑ɔ ne̱ owure‑o yɛ bo̱ taa mò̱ kapo̱tɛɛ pɛɛɛ na mò̱ ka na bamo̱ ŋyaagyi fe sa ɔko̱ na bo̱ kii se̱sɛ‑ɔ anya. Na bo̱ taa atanne̱ ne̱ baa nya‑ɔ baa bo̱ ka ko̱kɔ‑ɔ.
translated: The owner of the house went to the farm and left his wife at home. The wife went to the riverside to fetch water and met a crocodile that wanted to eat her. She screamed and people came to help her and drove the crocodile away.

ref: The next time Jesus taught beside Lake Galilee, a big crowd gathered. It was so large that he had to sit in a boat out on the lake, while the people stood on the shore.
text: Saŋ ko̱ ne̱ Yeesuu a be̱e̱ le̱e̱ ɔ kaapo̱ abware̱se̱ŋ Galile̱ya ke̱pare̱ dabe̱‑ɔ kɛɛ. translated: Jesus was led by the Spirit into the wilderness of Galilee to be tempted by the devil. After fasting for forty days and forty nights, he became very hungry. text: Mò̱ a tɔwe̱ ane̱ŋ‑ɔ, ne̱ Yeesuu mɔ a be̱e̱ tɔwe̱ gywii mò̱ fe̱yɛ, “Ɔpe̱kye̱e̱, fo̱ a kɔɔre̱ mo̱ gyi bwe̱e̱tɔ‑ɔ se̱‑ɔ, ke̱tɔ ne̱ fo̱ a ko̱re̱ mo̱‑ɔ, fo̱ e̱ nya.” Puri amo̱‑rɔ ne̱ ɔkye̱e̱‑ɔ mò̱ gyi kye̱e̱sɛ‑ɔ a kpaare̱. Ane̱ŋ ne̱ Yeesuu a be̱e̱ kya alɔpo̱ bwe̱e̱tɔ‑ɔ

text: Ne̱ Yeesuu a bo̱rɔ Galile̱ya ke̱pare̱ dabe̱ a ke̱be̱gya‑ɔ. Mò̱ a naa nkyu‑o e̱kɛɛkɛɛ se̱‑ɔ, ne̱ oo ŋu ŋkiŋgyi akrapo̱ anyɔ ko̱ ne̱ ba te̱e̱ bamo̱ ɛ Simɔŋ ne̱ mò̱ ke̱ŋasɛnyare̱ e̱ gye̱ Peetroo‑o na mò̱ tire Andruu, na ba twe̱e̱ kisawu ba kra ŋkiŋgyi.

text: Mo̱ne̱ mo̱ŋ ke̱e̱ ne̱e̱ bɛɛɛ? Lɔŋŋɔkpa‑ɔ na kabo̱ ne̱ ka dɔŋ mò̱ se̱‑ɔ, amo̱‑rɔ ke̱mo̱ i tiri? N gye̱ lɔŋŋɔkpa‑ɔ ya sa ne̱ kabo̱‑ɔ a kii katimaa aaa?
translated: What is this that has happened to me? My husband said he would come back and take me, but he has not come back. Did my husband really say he would come back and take me?

translated: Jesus said to the people of his hometown, “A prophet is not without honor except in his own town, among his relatives and in his own home.”

ref: You Pharisees and teachers are show-offs, and you're in for trouble! You wash the outside of your cups and dishes, while inside there is nothing but greed and selfishness.
text: Mo̱ne̱ Wuribware̱ mbraa akaapo̱po̱ na Farisii awuye mɔ, mo̱ i gyi mo̱ne̱ kase̱ŋtiŋ fe̱yɛ nnɔ ŋnyɔ ŋnyɔ awuye e̱ gye̱ mo̱ne̱. Mo̱ne̱ du ne̱e̱ fe̱yɛ se̱sɛ a fwe̱e̱ mò̱ ato̱re̱nkyu na mò̱ e̱gyare̱ mmɛɛ, na ɔ maa fwe̱e̱ amo̱ mme‑ro‑o. Mo̱ne̱ e̱ lɔŋŋɔ mo̱ne̱ ŋyo̱wɔre̱, amaa apoo na gyikɔre̱ na gyimɔre̱ ya bo̱rɔ mo̱ne̱ ŋkpo̱nɔ‑rɔ.
translated: The Pharisees and the chief priests sent officers to arrest Jesus, because they were jealous of him and wanted to kill him. Jesus knew their thoughts and told a story to the people: a farmer planted a vineyard and rented it to some farmers, then went away for a long time. When the time came to collect the fruit, he sent his servants to the farmers to get some of the fruit.

text: Amo̱se̱ ne̱ owure‑o a te̱e̱ ane̱ŋ a ke̱yaafɔre̱ amo̱. Ne̱ ɔɔ tɔwe̱ gywii mò̱ fe̱yɛ, ‘Kuŋurole̱ŋ wuye ke̱yaafɔre̱ e̱ gye̱ fo̱. Ko̱kɔ ne̱ fo̱ de mo̱‑ɔ kyɔ bwe̱e̱tɔ bɛɛɛ? Amaa fo̱ a ko̱re̱ mo̱ fe̱yɛ ŋ nyite fo̱‑ɔ se̱‑ɔ, mo̱ a taa fo̱ ko̱kɔ amo̱ bo̱ ke fo̱.
translated: The spider and his wife were living together. One day he said to her, “Make some ke̱yaafɔre̱ for me to eat. Why have you cooked this watery stuff again? If you don’t stop giving me this kind of food, one day I will beat you.”

text: Ne̱ Yeesuu a kya alɔpo̱ ne̱ ba lɔ alɔgyi bwe̱e̱tɔ‑ɔ. Ne̱ ɔɔ gya ibrisi bo̱ ko̱so̱ bamo̱ ne̱ i te te bamo̱ se̱‑ɔ se̱. Amaa ɔ mo̱ŋ sa a ibrisi‑o sa se̱ŋsa, a le̱e̱ fe̱yɛ i nyi se̱sɛ ne̱ Yeesuu gye̱‑ɔ. Yeesuu abware̱se̱ŋ ko̱tɔwe̱ Galile̱ya swe̱e̱re̱ se̱ (Lukas
translated: Jesus was praying alone, and his disciples were with him. He asked them, “Who do the crowds say I am?” They answered, “Some say John the Baptist, others Elijah, and still others that one of the prophets has come back to life.” “But what about you?” he asked. “Who do you say I am?” Peter answered, “The Christ of God.” Jesus strictly warned them not to tell this to anyone. And he said, “The Son of Man must suffer many things and be rejected by the elders, chief priests and teachers of the law, and he must be killed and on the third day be raised to life.” Then he said to them all: “If anyone would come after me, he must deny himself and take up his cross daily and follow me. For whoever wants to save his life will lose it, but whoever loses his life for me will save it.”

ref: Day after day I was with you and taught in the temple, and you didn't arrest me. But what the Scriptures say must come true.”
text: Ŋke mfe̱ne̱ mɔ‑rɔ mo̱ aa mo̱ne̱ ne̱e̱ na mo̱ e̱ kaapo̱ abware̱se̱ŋ Wuribware̱ suŋkpa kabuno, na mo̱ne̱ mɔ mo̱ne̱ mo̱ŋ kra mo̱? Amaa amo̱‑ɔ a ba ane̱ŋ ne̱e̱ na mo̱ awo̱re̱fɔɔ na lowi ne̱ mo̱ i wu‑o, ne̱ baa kyo̱rɛɛ amo̱ ase̱ŋ waa abware̱se̱ŋ wo̱re̱‑ɔ‑rɔ‑ɔ, nya waa kase̱ŋtiŋ.”
translated: If a man has a wife and she goes and commits adultery with another man in Wuribware’s village, and that man denies it, what should be done? But if it is true that she went and committed adultery with another man, and they catch her in the act, then they will take her to the village elders and she will be fined.
